what_it_does: A grounding/confirmation result for the B3-walkthrough-desktop.html mockup (a guided 'Walkthrough' view that auto-focuses one canvas item and dims the rest for a verdict). Holds 44 candidate dossiers reduced to 30 clusters, each with a ui:// address, capabilities, grounding flag, the what/what-you-can-do/how-to-change triple, a maps_to_feature id, and a 3-draw jury verdict sorting them into confirmed (9) and flagged (21).
observations: ground block describes the Walkthrough: system focuses attention on one item; operator can approve/reject/decide/comment/skip or speak a voice command.
Counts: n_candidates 44, n_dossiers 44, n_clusters 30 — the most clusters in the batch.

Confirmed: chat/run (unanimous), node-palette, process-11 spotlight, the memo-gate grounded-answer, walkthrough/force, walkthrough/camera (focus), verdict/approve (INB-resolve, unanimous, cluster_size 5), verdict/next, chat/inbox.
How-to text cites RhmChat.tsx, Walkthrough.tsx, runtime/suite.py:present_current, suite.py:resolve_surfaced, suite.py:walk_session, runtime/scheduler.py, and the /api/resolve wire.
Refcheck fabrications flagged: RHM-utterance, WALK-utterance, RHM-portal.
Distinct walkthrough verbs appear: force (bypass), ship-as-is, hold (pause to read), ask (pause-and-answer), idempotent (resolve once is safe), ground-check, operator-only verdict.
Address ui://chat/utterance appears on three separate dossiers; ui://chat/walk and ui://chat/toolbar each appear twice.
The idempotency dossier states resolving an item once is sufficient and safe.
standouts: A rich verb vocabulary for review: approve/reject/decide/comment/skip plus ship-as-is, hold, ask, force, next — the walkthrough as a structured verdict loop.
Idempotency guarantee surfaced as a UI affordance (resolve-once is safe).
Operator-only verdict gating — the verdict row is an operator-exclusive surface.
Memo-gate verdict shown with its own code reference (runtime/scheduler.py) as the skip justification.
conventions: Same audit schema as the rest of the batch (ground / counts / confirmed / flagged, howto triple, verdict object).
concerns: ui://chat/utterance reused across three dossiers and other addresses doubled — high address-collision risk in this surface.
21 of 30 clusters flagged; several 'built' controls (chat/toolbar, chat/walk) get 0-of-3 jury grounding.
RHM-utterance, WALK-utterance, RHM-portal all fail refcheck as non-inventory ids — multiple aspirational features the inventory lacks.
notes: The desktop Walkthrough slice of the UI-grounding audit; captures a structured operator-verdict review loop (approve/reject/decide/comment/skip + hold/ask/ship/force) with idempotency, but carries heavy address collisions and the most flagged clusters.
questions: How should the triply-reused ui://chat/utterance address be disambiguated across its three dossiers?
Should WALK-utterance / RHM-portal be added to the inventory or remapped to WALK-present / RHM-chat?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: an honest grounding audit of the guided-walkthrough verdict surface.
intention_signals: Aims to ground the guided-review walkthrough honestly — confirming the verdict and navigation controls that map to real resolve/present code while flagging the aspirational utterance/portal features and the unverifiable focus/state displays.
novelty: The walkthrough verb set (ship-as-is, hold, ask-mid-tour, force-with-bypass-prompt) and the idempotent resolve-once guarantee expressed as grounded UI affordances.
contribution: Contributes the walkthrough-desktop grounding-audit record: the guided-review verb inventory, idempotent/operator-only verdict semantics, and the confirmed/flagged verdicts a fused system would keep as the walkthrough grounding evidence.
summary_for_embedding: A UI-grounding audit result for the B3 desktop guided-Walkthrough mockup, clustering 44 dossiers into 9 confirmed and 21 flagged via refcheck and a 3-draw soft jury, covering a structured operator-verdict review loop.
intention_for_embedding: To verify each guided-walkthrough verdict and navigation control is truthfully grounded in real resolve/present code and flag aspirational features and address collisions for operator review.
